---
title: "SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures"
format:
  html:
    number-sections: false
---

{{< include _includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">Candidate Management Procedures: Specifications, Evidence, and Application</div>

::: {.status-note}
This site distinguishes **test candidates**, **shortlisted candidates**, and
**adopted management procedures**. Inclusion in the registry does not imply
endorsement or adoption.
:::

## Purpose

This site describes the candidate management procedures (CMPs) being evaluated
for South Pacific jack mackerel. It draws mainly on the current `jmMSE`
analysis and brings together:

1. the MP29 and MP32 candidate definitions advanced into robustness testing;
2. the full management strategy evaluation (MSE) process;
3. evidence from tuning, trade-off, and robustness comparisons; and
4. instructions for applying a selected procedure when new annual data become
   available.

SCW17 Papers 03, 04, 08, and 09 provide historical context and documentation
structure, but they do not define the current candidate registry.

The MSE calculations remain in
[`SPRFMO/jmMSE`](https://github.com/SPRFMO/jmMSE). This site records what each
CMP is, why it remains under consideration, how it would be applied, and which
analysis supports it.

## Start here

- [CMP registry](cmp/index.qmd): the two candidates currently advanced by
  `jmMSE`, MP29 and MP32.
- [MP29](cmp/mp29.qmd) and [MP32](cmp/mp32.qmd): candidate-specific rules and
  annual calculation details.
- [Annual application](application/annual-workflow.qmd): steps from receiving
  new data to producing advice.
- [Slick explorer](application/slick.qmd): view and compare MP29 and MP32 MSE
  results interactively.
- [How the MSE work fits together](workflow/mse-flow.qmd): where CMPs fit within the assessment
  and simulation system.
- [Evidence and history](evidence/scw17-evidence.qmd): current `jmMSE` evidence
  with SCW17 development context.
- [Sources and record of changes](governance/traceability.qmd): sources, review status, and a
  record of changes.

## Governing principle

The approved CMP specification is separate from background explanations and
MSE results. New scientific results may change whether a candidate is retained,
but they do not automatically change its data inputs, harvest control rule,
parameter values, catch-allocation method, or exceptional-circumstances rules.

## Terms used on this site

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| CMP | Candidate management procedure: the complete rule for turning agreed data into management advice. |
| HCR | Harvest control rule: the part of a CMP that converts a stock or index indicator into a catch or fishing-effort recommendation. |
| Operating model (OM) | A simulated description of the stock and fishery used to test a CMP. Alternative OMs represent different plausible assumptions. |
| Performance measure | A quantity used to compare CMPs, such as catch, spawning biomass, biological risk, or year-to-year catch variation. |
| IACC | Interannual catch change: a measure of how much catch changes from one year to the next. |
